reasoning: As of May 5, 2025, the phrase "snack foods" was still explicitly listed on the USDA's 'What Can SNAP Buy?' page. Although the new administration under Secretary Brooke Rollins and RFK Jr. has signalized a strong desire to restrict 'junk food' from SNAP, and although a historic waiver was signed on May 19, 2025, for Nebraska, that waiver was limited to soda and energy drinks in a single state. Since 'snack foods' remain legally eligible for purchase in all states (including Nebraska, for most snack items), removing the phrase from the national guidance website would potentially create confusion for retailers and participants. Federal website updates on major policy lists typically lag behind rhetoric and only change when broad, national policy or regulatory shifts are finalized. Given the phrase survived the first 100 days of the administration and the recent Nebraska waiver does not logically necessitate a change to the national 'snack foods' descriptor, it is more likely than not to still be present on July 1, 2025.
